sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline Miller, presents with a history of perioral dermatitis, rosacea, including ocular rosacea, which has been ongoing for an extended period.
She reports that her condition has improved significantly with the use of doxycycline, but has plateaued and not continued to improve since switching to once-daily dosing.
She has been using sulfacetamide wash twice a day and metrocream after washing her face, and has not used any other lotions or cleansers.
She denies any breast-feeding and has not used the elidel cream due to concerns about its safety.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Sulfacetamide wash, used twice a day
Metrocream, used after washing her face
Doxycycline, [DOSE NOT MENTIONED] taken once a day
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has Fitzpatrick skin type IV, with a few faint erythematous papules on bilateral medial cheeks and redness around and underneath her nostrils.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has perioral dermatitis, rosacea, including ocular rosacea.
Problem list: Perioral dermatitis; rosacea; ocular rosacea; unknown
Plan:
I recommended that the patient increase the use of doxycycline to twice a day and add the tacrolimus ointment, which I will prescribe.
I also instructed her to continue using the sulfacetamide wash and metrocream.
I will have her use the tacrolimus ointment until the rash is gone, then reduce to once a day for a week before stopping.
I also recommended that she continue using the metrocream and sulfacetamide wash after the rash has resolved.
Follow-up:
I scheduled a follow-up appointment in 4 weeks to assess the patient's progress and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
